13.reason: The format string "%d " has a trailing space, which may cause input issues; should be "%d"

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  The format string "%d " has a trailing space, which may cause input issues; should be "%d"
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  The format string "%d " expects a space after the integer, which may cause input issues; should be "%d"
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  The trailing space in scanf format can cause reading issues and does not match the input format. Also, no error handling for push when stack full (still executes the push after printing "error").
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The trailing space in the format string causes scanf to skip whitespace indefinitely, potentially causing the program to hang or misread subsequent inputs.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  The trailing space in the format string causes scanf to skip whitespace and wait for non-whitespace input, potentially causing the program to hang or misread input.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The format string has an extra space which may cause input parsing issues, especially at the end of input or before -1. This can lead to waiting for unintended input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The format string has an extra space which may cause input parsing issues, especially before -1; also, missing check before assignment leads to potential overflow]

16.reason: When the stack is full (top > 99), the push should not occur, but this line always executes, causing overflow

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  When the stack is full (top > 99), the push should not occur, but this line always executes, causing overflow
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  When the stack is full (top > 99), this line still pushes the element, causing overflow; should be in an else block
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  This line executes even when the stack is full (after printing "error"). Should be in an else block.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  should be inside else block
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Missing 'else' clause; this statement executes unconditionally, causing a push operation even when the stack is full, leading to buffer overflow.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This assignment happens even when the stack is full (i.e., after printing "error "), leading to buffer overflow. The push should only occur if not full.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Assignment happens even when stack is full (top > 99), causing buffer overflow]

21.reason: When the stack is empty (top == 0), this line still executes, causing underflow; also missing break statement

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  When the stack is empty (top == 0), this line still executes, causing underflow; also missing break statement
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  When the stack is empty (top == 0), this line still attempts to pop, causing underflow; should be in an else block
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  This line executes unconditionally. When stack is empty (top==0), it prints "error " and then attempts to access stack[-1], causing undefined behavior. Also, it should only execute in an else branch.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  missing break after case 0 (falls through to case -1); also executes even when empty
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Missing 'else' clause causes out-of-bounds array access when the stack is empty. Also missing 'break;' causes fall-through to case -1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Missing break statement causes fall-through to case -1. Additionally, if top is 0, accessing stack[-1] is out of bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [When the stack is empty (top == 0), this line still executes and accesses stack[-1], causing undefined behavior. The pop print should only happen if stack is not empty. Also, missing break causes fall-through to case -1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Pop operation executes even when stack is empty (after printing "error "), leading to undefined behavior]

23.reason: This break only exits the switch, not the while loop; should use return 0 to terminate

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  This break only exits the switch, not the while loop; should use return 0 to terminate
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  This break exits the switch, but the while loop continues, so the program does not stop when -1 is encountered. Should use return 0; or a loop exit mechanism.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  should be return 0; to exit program on -1
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  According to the problem, -1 indicates the end of operations. This should be return 0; to terminate the program, otherwise the while loop continues indefinitely or processes further input incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Missing break in case 0 causes fall-through to case -1, but more critically, after handling -1, the loop doesn't terminate immediately as it should. The program should exit upon reading -1.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Missing break after case 0 causes fall-through into case -1. Also, case -1 should terminate the program, but it only breaks the switch, not the while loop.
